title: Bruins to battle Buffalo Sabres in first round of Stanley Cup Playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins The Bruins will have a tough test against a Sabres team that has posted a record of 39-9-4 since Dec.
The Bruins will take on the Sabres in the first round of the Stanley Cup playoffs.

title: After budget letdown, hundreds rally for new Jackson Mann community center in Allston
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News After the mayor's five-year capital plan included $10 million for study into Jackson Mann's future, Councilor Liz Breadon is demanding $65 million “indicate that they're serious about building a community center here.
” More than a hundred people rallied at Jackson Mann to call for the city to fully fund a new community center.
Molly Farrar/Boston.

title: Lynn man arrested for robbery at Beverly mansion, charged with home invasion, kidnapping
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man was arrested after a two-week manhunt for a suspect who robbed one of the most expensive properties in Greater Boston.
An armed robbery occurred at a waterfront mansion in Beverly in March.
Danielle Parhizkaran/The Boston Globe A Lynn man was arrested Tuesday in connection with a break-in and armed robbery at a massive Beverly mansion, according to prosecutors.

title: Search of Mass. pond uncovers decades-old truck
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News "The Leicester Police Department will be investigating this further and coordinating the removal of the vehicle," the local fire department said.
A 40-year-old pickup truck was found in a Leicester pond Monday night after a fisherman’s sonar equipment detected the vehicle, fire officials said.

title: Driver who fatally struck Webster fourth-grader charged with OUI a week before
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime Just days before a Southbridge woman allegedly struck and killed a 10-year-old girl in a car crash, she stood before a judge for allegedly driving while on drugs.
Marleigh Guevara GoFundMe ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A Southbridge woman who allegedly struck and killed a 10-year-old girl in Webster on Saturday was arrested the week before for allegedly driving while on drugs, according to police.

title: 15-year-old arrested in shooting outside Dorchester Burger King
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News The teen is expected to face multiple charges for allegedly shooting another boy in the foot, police said.
By Carson Lyle April 14, 2026 | 7:17 PM 1 minute to read A 15-year-old boy was arrested Monday for allegedly shooting another boy in the foot outside of a Dorchester Burger King, police said.
While on patrol Monday afternoon, Boston police officers heard an apparent gunshot in the area of the Burger King.

title: This Army veteran will ruck Boston in honor of fallen comrades
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Boston Marathon "For me, this ruck is about honoring SSG Shaw and preserving the memory of a true hero who gave everything for his team," Fulton said.
Jeff Fulton will run the Tough Ruck Marathon on April 19.
Jeff Fulton By Carson Lyle April 14, 2026 | 5:13 PM 1 minute to read In our “Why I’m Running” series, Boston Marathon athletes share what’s inspiring them to make the 26.

title: Drake Maye: ‘Humbling’ Super Bowl loss is  motivation entering Year 3
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots "Everybody's got the same goal this time of year, to get back to that game," Maye said.

Drake Maye AP Photo/Julio Cortez The Patriots finished one win short of a Super Bowl title during Drake Maye’s second season, and the young quarterback offered some perspective on what he learned during a recent interview at The Masters.
Two months after the 29-13 loss to Seattle, Maye said he’s ready to continue his quest for the Lombardi Trophy.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her best friend
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Why I'm Running "This race is my way of honoring her strength, her resilience, and the love she showed everyone around her.
" By Kelly Chan April 14, 2026 | 4:00 PM 1 minute to read In our “Why I’m Running” series, Boston Marathon athletes share what’s inspiring them to make the 26.
2-mile trek from Hopkinton to Boston.

title: Boston Sand & Gravel says pedestrian deaths ‘inevitable’ if housing complex moves forward
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Business The company alleges a proposed apartment complex “will cause entirely foreseeable frequent injuries and deaths” and pose “great harm to the public.
” A lawsuit from Boston Sand & Gravel seeks to block a 705-unit housing development from being built along Rutherford Avenue.
David L.
